---
title: "CERDEV analysis report"
author: "Niccolò Mazzucco"
date: "2026-03-28"
output:
  html_document:
    toc: true
    toc_float: true
    number_sections: true
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(
  echo = TRUE,
  warning = FALSE,
  message = FALSE
)
```

This report documents the analytical workflow implemented in CERDEV.R for the CERDEV dataset.
The script reads the training and test datasets, performs variable cleaning and selection, explores wear-related variation through PCA and changepoint analysis, classifies wear classes through discriminant analysis, and estimates harvested straw counts, harvested surface, and crop yields.

```markdown
## Project structure
The analysis assumes the following folder structure:

```text
CERDEV/
├── CERDEV.R
├── CERDEV_report.Rmd
├── data/
│   ├── Supplementary Material_S1.xlsx
│   └── Supplementary Material_S2.xlsx
└── output/
```

## Project paths

```{r paths}
project_dir <- getwd()
data_dir <- file.path(project_dir, "data")
out_dir <- file.path(project_dir, "output")

training_input_file <- file.path(data_dir, "Supplementary Material_S1.xlsx")
test_input_file <- file.path(data_dir, "Supplementary Material_S2.xlsx")

project_dir
training_input_file
test_input_file
out_dir
```
## Output files generated

```{r cars}
list.files(out_dir)
```
## Input data
The script uses the following input files:

- data/Supplementary Material_S1.xlsx: training dataset
- data/Supplementary Material_S2.xlsx: test dataset


## Main analytical steps
Import of input datasets
Cleaning of the training dataset
Removal of constant and highly correlated predictors
Regression tree for working-time segmentation
PCA and changepoint analysis
Canonical discriminant analysis
Projection of experimental tools into discriminant space
Calibration of harvested straw counts
Conversion into harvested surface and estimated yields

## Output files
The script generates output files in the output/ folder, including figures, intermediate tables, and final prediction files.

## Reproducibility note
To reproduce the analysis, set the working directory to the CERDEV project folder and run CERDEV.R.

## Key Output Figures

### Figure 1. Regression tree
```{r fig-tree, echo=FALSE, out.width='80%'}
knitr::include_graphics(file.path(out_dir, "Figure_1.jpeg"))
```

### Figure 2. PCA loadings on PC1
```{r fig-tree, echo=FALSE, out.width='80%'}
knitr::include_graphics(file.path(out_dir, "Figure_2.png"))
```

### Figure 3. Segmented regression and changepoint analysis
```{r fig-tree, echo=FALSE, out.width='80%'}
knitr::include_graphics(file.path(out_dir, "Figure_3.png"))
```

### Figure 4. Standardized evolution of key surface parameters
```{r fig-tree, echo=FALSE, out.width='80%'}
knitr::include_graphics(file.path(out_dir, "Figure_4.png"))
```

### Figure 5. Boxplots of predictors by wear-time classes
```{r fig-tree, echo=FALSE, out.width='80%'}
knitr::include_graphics(file.path(out_dir, "Figure_5.jpg"))
```

### Figure 6. Canonical discriminant space of training surfaces
```{r fig-tree, echo=FALSE, out.width='80%'}
knitr::include_graphics(file.path(out_dir, "Figure_6.jpg"))
```

### Figure 7. Projections of experimental test tools
```{r fig-tree, echo=FALSE, out.width='80%'}
knitr::include_graphics(file.path(out_dir, "Figure_7.jpg"))
```

### Figure 8. Alternative calibration models (LD1 to cumulative harvest)
```{r fig-tree, echo=FALSE, out.width='80%'}
knitr::include_graphics(file.path(out_dir, "Figure_8.png"))
```

### Figure 9. Final harvesting model
```{r fig-tree, echo=FALSE, out.width='80%'}
knitr::include_graphics(file.path(out_dir, "Figure_9.png"))
```

### Figure 10. Harvesting model with experimental tools
```{r fig-tree, echo=FALSE, out.width='80%'}
knitr::include_graphics(file.path(out_dir, "Figure_10.png"))
```

### Figure 11. Combined figure of harvesting calibration and experimental tools
```{r fig-tree, echo=FALSE, out.width='80%'}
knitr::include_graphics(file.path(out_dir, "Figure_11.png"))
```

Output tables

The workflow also generates the following tabular outputs in the output/ folder:

1_selected_predictors.csv
2_selected_training_data_clean.csv
3_breakpoints_segmented_regression.csv
4_variables_contributions.csv
5_cross_table_results.csv
6_class_distribution_by_tools.csv
7_dominant_intervals_by_working_time_classes.csv
8_harvested_straw_prediction_tools.csv
9_harvested_surface_estimation.csv
10_tool_harvest_conversions.csv
Saved model object

The fitted harvesting model is also saved as:

Harvesting_model_fitted_linear.RDS

## Session information
```{r}
sessionInfo()
```
